🚨 ANOMALIES — 3 charts flagged for immediate review

  • 🚨 Δ STLFSI 4 weeks

  • ⚠ KRE / XLF

  • ⚠ SKEW / VIX
    The premium for sudden-drop insurance jumped about 10% in a single day relative to everyday market anxiety — that is a large one-session move and has pushed this ratio modestly above its recent normal. It suggests that p
  • ⚠ VIX / Realized Vol 30d
    The broad fear gauge fell roughly 13% today relative to how much the market actually moved over the past month — a sharp one-day compression that has pushed this reading below its recent normal. When expected fear runs t
  • ⚠ XLI / XLU

Aggregated bias · 1-glance synthesis
Today's verdict NEUTRAL-CAUTIOUS
When the signals disagree · and which one to trust
Different gauges sometimes point opposite ways. That is normal. Here is each clash in plain words, with the side to lean on for now.
⚠ These two disagree: Crash Index **2/10** (calm price action) and Regime State STABLE **13** vs VIX Spike tier **2/4** MEDIUM at **60%** with skew and backwardation both active, plus Mother Jaws **100** in TOPPING zone
Spot tape is quiet while the options market and cross-asset internals are paying for vol. Trust the options and divergence signals here. They lead, price confirms second.
⚠ These two disagree: Contrarian **50** ACCUMULATE (partial) vs Divergence composite TOPPING with **62.8%** of pairs diverging
One reading says lean in on weakness, the other says distribution is happening underneath. Trust divergence on the index level and use Contrarian only for single-name washouts.
Today's tension
Price action is calm while the options market is paying up for a vol expansion.
Bull
The Crash Index sits at **2/10** with the regime tagged STABLE at **13**, so the underlying tape has not broken. Contrarian reads **50** (ACCUMULATE partial), which says you fade panic, you do not chase it.
Bear
The VIX Spike Detector is at tier **2/4** with a **60%** probability, skew is hot, and the term structure is in backwardation. The divergence composite sits in the TOPPING zone at **62.8%** of pairs diverging with a Mother Jaws score of **100**, which is what a distribution top looks like before price confirms.
The tell: Watch whether VIX backwardation flips back into contango while skew cools. If it does, the bear case dies. If backwardation deepens and any T1 confluence trips, the bear case takes over.
AI read · Citrini · today's macro tape

The tape is calm on the surface and rotting underneath. Crash Index reads 2/10, Regime State is STABLE at 13/max, and Contrarian sits at 50 (partial ACCUMULATE). That is the quiet part. The loud part is the VIX Spike Detector at tier 2/4 with a 60% probability, both skew and backwardation active, and a divergence composite in the TOPPING zone with 62.8% of 43 pairs diverging and Mother Jaws at 100. Macro regime is BROADENING at only 30% confidence, so the rotation thesis is on probation.

For positioning, you respect both readings instead of picking one. Stay long the index at a reduced gross, because price has not broken and no T1 confluence is active (0 of 3). Hedge the tail with cheap convexity, because the options market is telling you it is not cheap by accident. Inside the book, lean away from crowded momentum and toward names where breadth has already cleaned out. The TOPPING divergence picture says you fade strength in the leaders, not weakness in the laggards.

The main risk is that one of the T1 confluences trips while NDX keeps leading lower. NDX is the US laggard at -5.1% over 5d while DJI only sits at -0.8%, which is exactly the kind of internal split that precedes a tier upgrade. The reading that flips the stance to defensive is a T1 x HYG or T1 x B2 confluence going active, or the VIX tier moving from 2/4 to 3/4. Until then, you trim, you hedge, you do not flatten.

5-regime heuristic classifier · cross-asset
Macro Regime — where are we structurally
AI read · macro regime

Regime is BROADENING at 30% confidence, which is the weakest possible version of a real broadening call. You position like rotation is happening without committing capital to it.

Take some chips off mega-cap momentum where mtum_spy correlation has flipped to +0.87 and rotate slowly into laggards with cleaner internals. Keep size honest until confidence climbs above the 50% mark or a T1 confluence forces the question.

SPY above its 200-DMA with contained vol. This is the zone where long convexity compounds: leverage and call buying carry the lowest variance drag. Size up your highest-conviction directional calls here.
Gate from Leverage for the Long Run (Gayed/Bilello 2016): the durable edge is the 200-DMA × vol regime, the 3× compounding is marketing. Attention gauge for call sizing, not a trade trigger.

Rates & Vol since April 2020 — yields surged across the board
Relative performance since April 2020 (rebased to the first April-2020 reading = 0%). When the everything-bubble started, US Treasury yields surged across the board off a near-zero base — bond volatility (MOVE) followed, while equity volatility (VIX) actually fell. The Kerberos tell: SPX vol stayed underpriced relative to bond vol.
UST-5Y yield+1030%UST-10Y yield+621%UST-30Y yield+291%MOVE · bond vol-19%VIX · SPX vol-72%
Yields: FRED DGS5/10/30 · MOVE/VIX: Yahoo ^MOVE/^VIX · single % axis (yields dwarf vol by design).

Dow Theory generalized · cross-asset divergences
Cross-asset divergence radar — Cross-asset divergence watch
AI read · divergences in plain English

The composite divergence is in the TOPPING zone with 62.8% of 43 pairs diverging, 21 bearish versus 6 bullish, net bias +15, and Mother Jaws at 100. In plain English, divergence means two things that usually move together have stopped doing so, and correlation drift is the speed of that change.

The cleanest tells: vix3m_vix moved from -0.32 to -0.83 and skew_vix from -0.20 to -0.80, so the vol complex is screaming internally. mtum_spy flipped from -0.51 to +0.87, which means momentum is no longer hedging the broad market, it is the broad market, and that is fragile.

xle_gld going from -0.81 to +0.22 says energy and gold are now trading like the same risk-off basket. This is the cross-asset version of leadership narrowing.

Cross-asset confluence · live status
Confluences — most proven alpha of the framework 0/3 ACTIVE
AI read · what the confluences mean now

Zero of three T1 confluences are active, so the bear setup has the pieces in place individually without the trigger chain. T1 x HYG (credit) is dormant, T1 x B2 (small caps) is dormant, T1 x JPY (yen safe-haven) is dormant.

You lean by staying engaged, not by going to cash. Add hedges while they are still cheap, keep gross down, and treat the first confluence print as the signal to cut net exposure, not the moment to start thinking about it.

✅
T1 × HYG (VIX backwardation + Credit stress)
Volatility backwardation meeting credit stress — the highest-conviction pre-correction setup in the framework. Fires only when options fear and the bond market confirm each other.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.190
🟢 HYG/IEF stressZ=+0.85
✅
T1 × B2 (VIX backwardation + Small-cap drawdown)
Volatility backwardation with small-caps breaking down — breadth thinning while vol rises. A late-rally warning when the index masks weakness underneath.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.190
🟢 IWM/SPY breakdownZ=+1.29
✅
T1 × JPY (VIX backwardation + Yen safe-haven flow)
Volatility backwardation with the yen bid — safe-haven flow confirming the vol signal. Classic risk-off rotation when carry trades unwind.
STAND-BY
🟢 VIX3M/VIX backwardation1.190

Sector rotation · 11 SPDR ETFs · 5 horizons
Sector Rotation Heatmap
Sorted by 21d performance (medium-term leadership). The rotation pattern reveals the regime: if XLU/XLP/XLV lead = defensive, if XLY/XLF/XLE lead = offensive cyclical. Sepia red→green gradient on returns.
Plain-English read · sector rotation
Which corners of the market lead tells you the market's mood better than the headline index. Defensive leadership (utilities, staples, health care) = investors playing safe. Cyclical leadership (discretionary, financials, energy) = appetite for risk. Over the last month, Financials, Tech, Healthcare lead. Leadership is mixed — no clean offensive or defensive signal this month.

SP500 top-60 cap-weighted · 5 horizons
SP500 Heatmap — leadership underneath the index
Top 60 stocks by market cap (~75% of S&P 500 weight). Sorted by 21d perf. Concentration pattern: if the top 5 do all the work, breadth dying. If rotation toward mid-caps of the list = broadening.
Plain-English read · what's under the index
The S&P can rise while most of its members fall, if a few giant stocks do all the lifting. This ranks the 60 biggest names by one-month move. Gains spread down the list = healthy breadth; gains stuck in the top few = a narrow, fragile rally. Right now 33 of the top 60 are up over the month — participation is split.

🌍
Asia · Europe · US · Crypto 24/7 — lead-lag detection
Cross-Region Surveillance
Major indices from the 4 regions/asset classes. Asia trades ahead time-wise vs US — a Tokyo overnight crash often leaks into the SPX open. Crypto = 24h leading indicator of global risk appetite.
AI read · the cross-region tape

The US is the weakest tape with NDX at -5.1% over 5d while DJI is only down -0.8%, so the pain is concentrated in tech and the rest of the index is hanging in. Asia mirrors that split with KOSPI at -11.7% versus AXJO at -1.2%, which says the semis and tech-heavy benchmarks are leading the bleed globally.

Europe is the cleanest with CAC at +0.6% and DAX at -1.5%. Crypto is grinding with BTC at -3.3% and ETH at -7.2%, which tracks the same growth-asset selloff.

For the US cash open, you expect NDX to keep underperforming DJI until the divergence picture cools, and you size accordingly.

01
Forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
Crash Index — 10 lead-signal radar 1/10 · WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
AI read · volatility & crash, reconciled

Crash Index at 2/10 and VIX Spike tier at 2/4 is not a contradiction, it is the normal sequence. The Crash Index reads realized price damage and current regime, so it lags.

The Spike Detector reads what dealers and end users are paying for in options, with skew active and the VIX curve in backwardation at a 60% spike probability. Backwardation means near-dated VIX futures trade above longer-dated ones, which only happens when the market expects vol now, not later.

You trust the options tape here because it leads by days, and the calm Crash print is the consolation prize you get for hedging while it is still possible.

Plain-English read · the Crash Index
Think of this as a smoke alarm, not a market forecast. Each of the 10 rows is a signal that led real crashes, checked over 15 years of S&P 500 history (Bonferroni-validated, so it survived a strict false-positive test). In a calm market it sits near zero by design — a low number is the alarm working, not a broken gauge. It only earns your attention when 3 or more rows light up together. Today it reads 1/10, so the tape is calm and there is no crash setup.
CRASH INDEX
1/10
WATCH — 1-2 signals, monitoring
10 forward-looking indicators · Bonferroni-validated
DXY z>1.5 structural
lead 30-90d ❌ z=+0.89
DXY+SKEW combo structural
lead 45-346d ❌
SKEW>140 (Tail Risk) structural
lead 1-94d ✅ 142.7
SKEW>155 structural
current regime ❌ 142.7
VIX3M/VIX<1.0 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ 1.19
STLFSI4 d20 stress
lead 5-30d ❌ -0.22
HYG/IEF z<-1.5 stress
lead 5-20d ❌ z=+0.85
T10Y2Y resteepen stress
recession warning ❌ -0.13
SPY -2% in 5d trigger
lead 5-15d ❌ +1.80%
VIX +30% in 3d trigger
lead 1-10d ❌ -15.59%
Score 0-2 CALM · 3-4 WATCH · 5-6 WARNING · 7-8 ALARM · 9-10 EXTREME (rare crash imminent). Lead times: structural 30-90d · stress 5-30d · triggers 1-10d. Four Telegram alerts available depending on escalation.
How to read
Each row = an independent signal Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY. The structural (DXY, SKEW, MOVE) signal a regime change over 30-90 days. The stress (HYG/IEF, VIX3M/VIX, STLFSI4) ring at 5-30 days. The triggers (SPY -2% in 5d, VIX +30% in 3d) are short-term breakouts. For a tactical decision: 3+ signals simultaneously aligned = real probability of stress, never on isolated signal (framework rule).
02
Regime score · 6 bearish signals (Bonferroni + walk-forward)
Regime State 9 · STABLE — no regime shift
9
STABLE — no regime shift
Aligned 3+d 4/20
Yield steepening 0/20
MOVE Z-score 5/20
STLFSI momentum 0/20
Credit stress 0/20
🆕 DXY surge 0/20
Plain-English read · the regime
This score answers one question: is the big-picture backdrop turning against stocks? It adds up 6 sub-signals that have each predicted the S&P lagging. Below 50 means no regime reason to de-risk; above 50 means the weather has changed. At 9/100 it is calm — nothing on the regime side argues for cutting risk. The only flicker right now is the bond-market fear gauge (MOVE) (5/20), and on its own that is not enough to flip the regime.
How to read
Score 0-100 aggregated from 6 sub-signals (each 0-20) that predict SPY underperformance. Bonferroni-validated over 15 years, walk-forward robust. Above 50 → active-bearish regime (trim winners, tight stops). DXY surge added in May 2026 (current regime 2023-2026, strong dollar = toxic for global risk-on).
03
Contrarian score · 3 buy signals (Bonferroni + walk-forward)
Contrarian Buy 48 · ACCUMULATE — partial signal
48
ACCUMULATE — partial signal
VIX capitulation 0/50
VIX3M/VIX = 1.19 (backwardation = capitulation)
Inflation regime 48/50
corr SPY-TLT 60d = 0.48
🆕 SMH BTFD (regime current) 0/50
SMH/SPY Z=+1.77 (<-1.5 → +11% 60d test 2023-26)
Contrarian Score = signals that predict SPY OUTPERFORMANCE (capitulation buy). VIX backwardation → +7.29pp SPY at 120d · Corr SPY-TLT>0 → +3.88pp at 120d · SMH z<-1.5 → +11pp at 60d (test 2023-2026). Reading: score > 70 = aggressive fear-buy setup.
How to read
Bonferroni MCPT backtest 2026-04-30 over 15 years SPY · 9/15 tests VALID. Regime Score vs Contrarian Score are independent — they can coexist high (regime transition) or low (structural calm). Always read the combination, not each one alone.
04
VALID signals · live snapshots
framework rule — Max Optimism · RSI Breadth · xMarketTopMas · Composite Divergence
Plain-English read · what the crowd is doing
These panels read the crowd, not the price: how stretched option bets are, how loved high-beta is versus safe assets, and how many stocks are overbought or oversold. The rule is the same as everywhere else on this page — one stretched gauge is just noise; you only act when several agree. Optimism stack: 1 of its components are stretched. Breadth zone: neutral. Extremes here flag where to be careful, not when to trade — wait for confluence.
framework rule · validated signals live snapshots — Bonferroni-validated, surfaced from lib/ + Streamlit pages

How to read
Four validated signals from the framework (libs kerberos_*), surfaced here so the cockpit reflects what the doctrine flags today: Max Optimism Stack (4 sentiment components + 3d buffer + haven filter) · RSI Breadth zones (capitulation vs euphoria) · xMarketTopMas (high-beta vs duration risk appetite) · Composite Divergence (cross-asset pairs diverging). Full stats on the Backtest page.

3 confluence components (Validated, regime 2023-2026)
1. SKEWACTIVE
142.7
Trigger: > 140 (tail-hedge demand)
2. BackwardationACTIVE
VIX3M/VIX = 1.190
Trigger: > 1.05 (vol curve inverted)
3. Adaptive (proxy)INACTIVE
VIX 16.4 · ratio 1.19
Trigger: VIX < 16 AND ratio > 1.15
Probability VIX +50% within 60d
60%
vs baseline 37% · backtested cycle 2023-2026
Action — sizing tied to tier
Buy hedges B-tier (1-2% book) — VIX calls 30-60 DTE strike 25-28 OR SPY puts 60-90 DTE Δ0.20-0.30
How to read
4 tiers (BROAD / MEDIUM / HIGH / EXTREME) based on the confluence of 3 Validated components: SKEW > 140 (tail-hedge demand), VIX3M/VIX > 1.05 (vol curve inverted), VIX < 16 + ratio > 1.15 (adaptive proxy). Each tier indicates a historical probability of VIX +50% at 60d (cycle backtest 2023-2026, vs baseline 37%). Strict sizing: tier 2 = 1-2% book hedges, tier 3 = 3-5% long-vol, tier 4 = 5-10% MAX (rare confluence).
05b
Under-the-hood vol · Observation (Imran Lakha)
Vol Microstructure — VIX beta & term structure
VIX spot
16.32
VIX beta 20d
-9.31
0th pct
VIX beta 60d
-3.17
0th pct
VIX9D / VIX
0.949
contango
VIX3M / VIX
1.203
contango
VVIX / VIX
5.57
vol-of-vol
VIX beta 20d collapsed (-9.31, 0th pct) — historically VIX becomes a buy. BUT only if VIX catches a bid on the next spot down-move. If VIX stays asleep, the setup is dead (Lakha's caveat).
How to read
VIX beta = how much VIX moves per unit SPX move (rolling regression). Collapsed beta (low pct) = VIX asleep → historically a buy, but worthless unless VIX re-engages on the downside. VIX9D/VIX > 1 & VIX3M/VIX < 1 = backwardation (stress). Observation only — no sized signal.

06
Aggregated bias · 53-ratio composite
Regime synthesis — RISK-ON / OFF / STRESS / TOPPING
MIXED — competing signals No clean composite per Kerberos. Wait for alignment.
RISK-ON aligned (3)
• Small caps leading
• Copper > Gold
• Industrials > Utilities
RISK-OFF aligned (1)
• ETH dumping vs BTC
STRESS aligned (0)
none
TOPPING aligned (0)
none
How to read
Aggregation of all panel signals across the 4 cross-asset buckets. RISK-ON = risk appetite (cyclical, equity, EM). RISK-OFF = flight to defensives (TLT, gold, JPY). STRESS = liquidity tension (HYG, MOVE, STLFSI). TOPPING = end-of-cycle signals (concentration, breadth narrowing, defensives leading). Doctrine: a single signal = noise. 3+ aligned in one bucket = real confirmed regime.

Dow Theory — Transports must confirm Industrials
0.4268 -1.33%

The ratio is still slightly above its recent norm, but a one-day drop this sharp deserves attention. Watch whether transports keep slipping over the next week while the rest of the market holds up.

Check back in a few days to see whether it stabilises or keeps drifting lower.

Gold vs Equity — haven flow

If gold continues to gain ground while stocks wobble, that would signal real protective buying is beginning. Right now it is an early nudge, not a confirmed alarm.
KRE / XLF
Stretch +0.11
Regional banks vs Big banks — credit health
1.3338 -1.10%
◆ AI READ · WARNING · 2026-06-15
⚠️ Smaller regional banks dropped 2% today compared to the big Wall Street names — a sharp single-day gap that matches the early warning pattern seen before the 2023 banking stress. The overall ratio is still modestly above its recent norm, but this kind of sudden underperformance is exactly how credit cracks have historically announced themselves before anyone else noticed. Watch this over the next few trading days.
QQQ / SPY
Stretch +1.60
Tech leadership — must confirm broad rally
0.9727 -1.31%
◆ AI READ · ANOMALY · 2026-06-15
🚨 Technology stocks surged another 1.35% above the broader market today, pushing this ratio well outside the high end of its normal 100-day range — a historically rare, stretched reading. When virtually all the market's gains are concentrated in a small group of giant tech companies, the rally becomes fragile: a stumble in just those few names can drag everything lower quickly. This is a reading worth watching very closely right now.
BTC / SPY
Stretch -1.70
Crypto leading equity (24/7 risk gauge)
87.8109 +2.51%
◆ AI READ · WATCH · 2026-06-15
👀 Bitcoin slipped slightly today and remains well below where it has typically traded relative to stocks over the past 100 days. This stretched gap can read one of two ways: either crypto already sent an early risk-off signal that stocks have not yet priced in, or Bitcoin is coiled for a sharp catch-up move if sentiment shifts. A decisive break above recent highs in Bitcoin would be a positive signal for broader risk appetite.
RSP / QQQ
Stretch -1.20
Equal-weight vs Tech (concentration check)
0.2907 +1.60%
◆ AI READ · WARNING · 2026-06-15
⚠️ The average stock fell 2.5% behind the big technology names today — one of the largest single-day gaps in recent months. When nearly all of the market's gains come from a handful of giant companies while the rest of the market fades, participation is dangerously narrow and rallies built on that foundation tend to be fragile. Watch whether broader stocks start reclaiming ground or whether more names begin to roll over in the days ahead.

Black swan insurance

This level has been the norm for recent months, so it is not yet alarming, but worth tracking if it climbs further. A continued rise would suggest quiet unease building beneath the surface.

⚠️ The premium for sudden-drop insurance jumped about 10% in a single day relative to everyday market anxiety — that is a large one-session move and has pushed this ratio modestly above its recent normal. It suggests that protection against rare, sharp drops is getting more expensive faster than routine worries. Watch whether this keeps climbing over the next few days.
VIX / Realized Vol 30d
Stretch -1.23
Vol regime persistence — chop vs crash detector
1.15 +0.69%
◆ AI READ · WARNING · 2026-06-15
⚠️ The broad fear gauge fell roughly 13% today relative to how much the market actually moved over the past month — a sharp one-day compression that has pushed this reading below its recent normal. When expected fear runs this close to actual recent swings, it sometimes signals that calm is being taken for granted ahead of a volatility surprise. Watch whether the market continues moving quietly or starts picking up faster than the fear gauge implies.

CREDIT SPREADS · YIELD CURVE
Credit & rates — risk-on/off proxies
HYG / IEF
Stretch +0.85
High yield credit stress
0.8467 -0.27%
◆ AI READ · HEALTHY · 2026-06-15
✅ Riskier corporate bonds are holding steadily above government bonds, sitting modestly above their recent normal — investors remain comfortable taking on credit risk. Almost no movement today reinforces the calm picture.
LQD / IEF
Stretch +1.07
Investment grade liquidity
1.1545 -0.13%
◆ AI READ · HEALTHY · 2026-06-15
✅ High-quality corporate bonds remain in a healthy position relative to government bonds, with today's tiny dip leaving the overall picture unchanged. No signs of stress in credit markets.
T10Y2Y
Stretch -1.78
Yield curve (10y - 2y)
0.38 -5.00%
◆ Plain-English read
Difference between 10-year and 2-year Treasury yields. Below zero, the curve is inverted — historically a recession signal within 12 to 24 months. The exact timing reveals itself when the curve steepens violently (re-steepening = effective start of the recession). Current reading: +0.38 — positive curve, no recession signal. No inversion in progress. Action: no macro defensive reading from this curve.
T10Y3M
Stretch +0.21
Fed-preferred curve (10y - 3m)
0.64 -5.88%
◆ Plain-English read
Slope between 10 years and 3 months — it's the Fed's preferred curve, considered historically the most reliable recession indicator (better than T10Y2Y). Current reading: +0.64 — no recession trigger. The Fed curve is neither inverted nor in strong re-steepening. Action: no imminent recession signal.
TIP / TLT
Stretch -0.17
Inflation expectations
1.2735 -0.55%
◆ AI READ · HEALTHY · 2026-06-15
✅ The bond market's signal for inflation fears sits right in the middle of its normal range with almost no movement today. Nothing unusual is happening here — inflation anxiety in bonds is quiet.
MOVE
Stretch -0.58
Bond volatility (BofA)
67.3 -3.00%
◆ AI READ · QUIET · 2026-06-15
💤 Bond market volatility is calm and unremarkable, sitting well away from any stress threshold that would signal trouble ahead.
Corr SPY / TLT 60d
Stretch +1.30
Equity-bond regime
0.48 -10.90%
◆ AI READ · WARNING · 2026-06-15
⚠️ Stocks and long-term government bonds have been moving in the same direction for the past two months, and this relationship is stretched well above where it normally sits. This matters because when stocks sell off, you usually want bonds to rise as a cushion — right now that cushion is much thinner than usual, so watch for any sign this unusual lock-step movement breaks.
Δ T10Y2Y 60d
Stretch -0.44
Yield curve steepening rate
-0.13 +18.18%
◆ Plain-English read
Change in T10Y2Y slope over 60 days. A strong positive change = the curve steepens rapidly, a dangerous pattern when it was coming out of an inversion (= often the start of recession). Current reading: Δ -0.13 over 60d — neutral change. No recession signal from the curve movement.

11
COMPOSITES · STRESS GAUGES
Sentiment & stress — confluence radar
Plain-English read · sentiment & stress
This stacks the fear-and-greed gauges side by side: a bank-system stress index (STLFSI), the retail bull-minus-bear survey (AAII), and the options put/call ratio. One gauge alone lies often; when they agree you get a real read on whether the crowd is greedy or scared. Greed extremes warn you to trim and hedge; fear extremes are where contrarian buys live.
STLFSI4
Stretch -1.40
Systemic financial stress
-0.868 +26.21%
◆ AI READ · WARNING · 2026-06-12
⚠️ Financial stress across U.S. markets is still comfortably below average — conditions are calm by historical standards. However, the reading jumped roughly 26% in a single day, which is a unusually large one-day swing even from a low starting point. Watch over the next few days whether this is a one-off blip or the beginning of stress slowly creeping back toward normal.
Δ STLFSI 4 weeks
Stretch -0.76
Financial stress momentum
-0.22 +691.04%
◆ AI READ · ANOMALY · 2026-06-12
🚨 The measure tracking how much financial stress has shifted over the past four weeks made a nearly seven-times larger move in a single day — that kind of acceleration in the rate of change is genuinely rare. The four-week direction still shows conditions improving overall, but today's outsized jump in momentum is the sort of early-warning signal that warrants close attention. If this keeps rising over the next several sessions, it would suggest the recent calm period may be winding down.

Retail sentiment spread
📊
AAII weekly survey (Bullish % − Bearish %). >+30 = complacency, <-20 = capitulation. Manual entry from aaii.com.

When crypto investors pile into Bitcoin and avoid Ethereum like this, it often reflects a cautious mood across riskier assets broadly. The small bounce today is a start, but the gap remains unusually wide — watch whether Ethereum continues to recover.

Best Risk/Reward Names — top EV% with active screener signal
Ticker Theme Tier Screener R/R EV% 52w%
LUNRSpace RaceT3PULLBACK8.29x22.5%63%
POETPhotonicsT3BLOWOFF5.72x20.6%52%
RDWSpace RaceT3BLOWOFF2.18x19.3%59%
BKSYSpace RaceT3PULLBACK16.93x18.7%65%
RKLBSpace RaceT1BREAKOUT8.76x16.2%68%
FLNCBatteriesT3BOTTOM1.60x15.8%65%
AAOIPhotonicsT1PULLBACK2.56x15.5%72%
PLUGEnergy GridT3PULLBACK7.75x15.1%64%
EV% = win_rate × (target−entry) − (1−win_rate) × (entry−stop). Tier = T1 highest conviction. 52w% = % of 52-week high. Entry/stop/target levels live on the Trade Desk →
How to read
Heat ranking: composite of RS (relative strength vs SPY), Momentum (price action 20-60d), Breadth (% stocks above SMA), Options flow, and Attention. Use top-5 as rotation focus.
Emerging industries: high velocity = screener scores accelerating this week vs prior baseline — potential early rotation signal.
Competitor themes: token frequency × recency across top finance Substacks — useful narrative context, not a trading signal.
Best R/R: only entries with an active screener leg (PULLBACK / BOTTOM / SMART_MONEY_SURGE etc.). EV% = probabilistic edge per trade. Cross-check with weekly chart before sizing.

Reading the table — Z-Score: number of standard deviations vs rolling 100d mean. |Z| > 2 = Kerberos entry threshold, |Z| > 2.5 = extreme (best R/R on mean-reversion). %B: position within Bollinger Bands (1.0 = upper band, 0 = lower band).

Aggregate reading — the mean of Z-scores gives a global regime indicator. Positive → cross-asset overbought market (Risk-On, watch reversal). Negative → oversold (Risk-Off, possible bottoming).
How to read
Persist = number of consecutive days above |Z| > 2.0 (Kerberos threshold). Persist ≥ 3d = validated signal (the divergence has become a regime, not a spike). Persist 1-2d = to watch. Persist 0d = dead signal. Sort on this column to find established regimes worth trading.
Glossary — 18 terms to understand the dashboard
Z-Score
Number of standard deviations vs the historical mean (rolling 100 days). Z=0 = normal level · |Z|>2 = stress · |Z|>2.5 = extreme.
Bollinger Bands
Bands drawn at ±2.2σ around a 100-day moving average. When the price exits the bands = statistically extreme signal.
Backwardation
Inverted curve: immediate fear exceeds far fear (e.g. VIX > VIX3M). Short-term panic signal.
Contango
Normal situation where far fear exceeds immediate fear. Extreme contango = complacency.
Half-life
Number of days required for a gap to the mean to halve. Measures the speed of mean-reversion.
Cointegration
Two assets that move together in the long term despite short-term fluctuations. Statistical basis of pair-trading.
Mean-reversion
Strategy betting that a gap to the mean will close. Inverse of momentum (which bets on continuation).
Breadth
Width of participation in the rally. Good breadth = many stocks rising. Bad breadth = only a few mega-caps pull the index.
Hedge ratio
Quantity of asset B to short to neutralize 1 long unit of asset A. Calculated via OLS regression on historical returns.
Lead-time
Delay between a signal's appearance and the event it predicts. Structural = 30-90d · stress = 5-30d · triggers = 1-10d.
Bonferroni
Statistical correction for multiple testing. When testing 100 signals, some seem valid by chance. Bonferroni divides the significance threshold by the number of tests to eliminate these false positives.
MCPT (Monte Carlo Permutation Test)
Robustness test: data is randomly shuffled 5000-10000 times to see if the signal appears by chance. If p < 0.05, the signal is probably real.
Walk-forward
Test where the model is trained on the first years (e.g. 2010-2020) and tested on the following years (2020-2025). Avoids in-sample overfitting.
Tail-hedge
Hedge against an extreme crash (-20% or more). Bought via deeply out-of-the-money puts. SKEW measures institutional demand for this type of insurance.
Mega-caps
The 7-10 largest market caps in the SPX (AAPL, MSFT, NVDA, GOOGL, AMZN, META, TSLA). Weigh ~35% of the index.
Cap-weighted vs Equal-weighted
SPY = weighted by capitalization (mega-caps dominate). RSP = each stock weighs 0.2%. Comparing both reveals concentration.
Risk parity
Portfolio strategy that balances risk (not capital) across asset classes. Stressed when stocks/bonds correlations turn positive (inflation regime).
framework rule
Fundamental principle: a solo signal = noise, 3+ aligned signals = real edge. All panel signals are Bonferroni-validated over 15 years of SPY.
